nativeRange.habitat: Trade cultivar collected from Vietnam. Lineage variously attributed to the Rotala rotundifolia complex, Rotala wallichii, or an undescribed Rotala. The 'Rotala Vietnam' umbrella label encompasses this and adjacent collected forms.

decorPreferences: Nutrient-rich aquasoil substrate with iron-rich root tabs
High light (PAR 60+) for the signature orange-pink to burgundy-red top growth
CO2 injection generally required for tight bushy form and saturated color
Soft acidic water tends to deepen the red coloration
Hedge-style trimming to maintain a dense carpet of colored tops

summary: Vietnamese trade cultivar of Rotala prized for its sunset orange-pink to burgundy-red top growth under high-tech conditions. Lineage is debated within the rotundifolia complex. A demanding background stem with iron-sensitive coloration.
careNotes:
Rotala 'H'Ra' is a Vietnamese-collected trade cultivar widely used in Dutch-style and competition aquascapes for its sunset-hued top growth, ranging from orange-pink through deep burgundy under strong light. Lineage is unsettled: some sources position it as a cultivar of Rotala rotundifolia, others align it with Rotala wallichii, and a few treat it as an undescribed Rotala from the rotundifolia complex. The 'Rotala Vietnam' umbrella term in the hobby trade is commonly applied to this cultivar and adjacent Vietnamese-origin Rotala forms.

Care is generally more demanding than common R. rotundifolia: best results tend to require high light (PAR 60+), reliable CO2 injection, iron-rich macro and micro dosing, soft acidic water, and regular trimming. Under low light or low CO2, color has been reported to revert to olive-green and the cultivar loses its distinguishing feature. The plant has a feathery, pine-needle-like texture and propagates via lateral shoots and stem cuttings, with a creeping growth habit via side shoots. Hedge-trim regularly to keep a tight carpet of colored tops near the back glass.

DATA STATUS NOTE: Researched 2026-06-12. Only the primary source (Aquarium Co-Op product page) responded with care data. Secondary sources Buce Plant (404), Modern Aquarium (no product page found), 2Hr Aquarist (403 forbidden), and Tropica catalog (not listed) all failed. Wikipedia 'Rotala' redirects to a UK bus company (genus article is at 'Rotala (plant)'). Water parameters, sizing, and substrate/fertilization specifics are inherited from the Rotala rotundifolia complex baseline and should be verified by reviewer.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Rotala H'Ra
primary.notes: Confirmed: stem plant, midground/background placement, high light, CO2 supplementation needed, creeping growth via side shoots, sunset orange/pink/red coloration under high light, feathery pine-needle-like texture, suited to high-tech planted tanks.
